documents.title: Time Off Request
documents.body:
# Time Off Request

1. Submit via the [Time Off Request Form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScgV0Owf2Vex-D9uQwG8tP12ZKbBcVPMYcCblSBltthCk6img/viewform)
2. Approvals: Tad
3. Block calendar and notify your team

documents.title: MNDA
documents.body:
# MNDA Process

Use when a prospect or partner needs a mutual NDA before sharing materials.

1. Log the request in the [Agreement Dashboard](#engagements/agreements)
2. Send Larx standard MNDA (see Sales Process & Assets)
3. Update status when signed

documents.title: New Hire Onboarding
documents.body:
# Onboarding

1. Set up accounts
2. Meet the team
3. Review active engagements

documents.title: Partnerships
documents.body:
# Partnership Agreements

For formal partnership or reseller agreements.

1. Qualify the partner — align with GTM lead
2. Track in the [Agreement Dashboard](#engagements/agreements)
3. Legal review before signature
4. Add partner to Engagements → Partners tab once active

documents.title: Company
documents.body:
# Who and What Are We?

**Purpose:** Enable high-stakes enterprise teams to survive in an information-overloaded environment.

**Larx, in one sentence:** A cognitive system for entire teams that operate in mission-critical environments.

## Offices

**Larx, Inc.** (Parent)
3131 Piedmont Road, Suite 200
Atlanta, GA 30305

**Larx AI Ltd.** (Europe)
Manfield House, 1 Southampton Street
London, WC2R 0LR

documents.title: Demos
documents.body:
# Demo Process

## Request & schedule

- Confirm POC, attendees, and use case with sales lead
- See **Engagement Demo Prep** for the runbook

## After the demo

- Submit feedback via the [Demo Feedback Form](https://forms.gle/HYc56mQF1iUMEdKz8)
- Log next steps in Larx Hub Engagements within 24h

documents.title: Pilots
documents.body:
# Pilot Programs

## Tracking

- Master list: [Pilot Programs sheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1R9OaACKjdDbQoD_hNpFXclV9xwKqpo8NGMVUne1Fruc/edit)
- Operational detail: Larx Hub → **Engagements** (status, POC, next step, attachments)

## Lifecycle

1. Qualify → log as prospect engagement
2. MNDA signed → move to active pilot
3. Weekly updates in HubSpot and Engagements
4. Close → completed or paused with notes

meetings.title: Weekly Pipeline Review
meetings.summary:
### Pipeline Updates by Rep

- Marae: outbound-focused week, using Claude as SDR/BDR
  - 10 new companies/day, 1-2 contacts each, personalized emails
  - Multi-threaded outreach: email, LinkedIn, calls
  - HubSpot segment created (“commercial”) for Claude to populate daily
  - Call today with one of Brian’s contacts to validate Larx commercial messaging
- Rory: heavy framework and BD activity across UK/MOD
  - YouForce: hardware install pending, draft trial scope ready to send to Adam and Rob
  - 30 Commando EW deployment: gone quiet, chaser sent
  - Picasso framework (UK gov space tech): Fujitsu likely to win, re-engaging post September down-select
  - Asgard: meeting booked 27th with Ethic; Oracle meeting booked to explore D-DAD framework access
  - Digital Targeting Web: first contract turn done (API management), next challenge (AI decision intelligence) expected September
  - Thames Water: new lead, warrant officer contact across 14 sites, exploring security coordination use case
  - Task Force RAID: 100M GBP AI procurement vehicle, pursuing access
  - Project Strong: sovereign agentic decision support, identified and tracking
  - Futures Lab: application stalled, chased again today
  - Effort split: 50% YouForce, 50% UK gov; zero Europe currently due to no travel budget
  - Europe targets if travel unlocked: Nordics, Poland, Estonia, Germany
- Colby: US Navy pipeline, all out of San Diego
  - PMW 790 (technical director) and PMW 120 (C4/C5 ISR) in early conversations
  - SURFDEVRON J2: former NSW Group 8 advocate, now at surface unmanned battalion, in-person demo planned
  - PMS 408 demo done yesterday: directed to fill out forms at EOD Unit 3 to initiate funding/RCO approval
  - EOD Unit 3 contact (Joey, ops master chief) can complete paperwork
  - HubSpot not connected yet; all outreach done manually

### Reuters Pilot: July 20th Launch Plan

- Core issue: production is behind dev; remote object store capability (S3, GCP) fully rebuilt in dev, not yet in production
- Three options discussed:
  1. Proceed as-is on old production, frozen for 8 weeks
  2. Lift deployment gate, push dev to production mid-pilot (requires retraining)
  3. Delay pilot 1-2 weeks to let dev stabilize
- Decision: push kickoff to July 20th, get credentials tomorrow instead
  - Tomorrow’s call scope: security/compliance and credential handoff only, nothing else
  - Credentials likely an encrypted auth/bearer token via email; Proton to be used as transport
  - Colter to attend tomorrow in Colby’s place (familiar face, met Sarah at GEOINT); Allison also attending
  - Colby to send pre-call email to Colter, Rob, and Reuters distro confirming scope and July 20th start date

### HubSpot Activity Gaps

- Dashboard showing zeros across the board for activity this week
- Rory: HubSpot captures ~50% of his activity; frameworks don’t fit the deals model until contract stage
- Colby: nothing connected, all outreach done manually, needs full HubSpot hookup
- Marae’s commercial segment may not be pulling into dashboards correctly; Allison to review connections post-call
- Going forward: tracking deals, contracts, and raw activity/outreach separately
  - Email and call connections needed so weekly touch-point volume is visible

### Pilot Process and Feedback Loop

- Historical pilots: feedback relayed via Slack to engineering channel, unclear if actioned
- New standard going forward:
  - Rob and Andres to join all customer feedback calls directly (no telephone game)
  - Capture user category per pilot: operator, decision-maker, or analyst
  - Engineer looped into every pilot for feedback quality and relevance to dev roadmap
  - Pilot vs. showcase distinction: logged-in users with structured feedback vs. spectators
- Rob flagged: production has a large number of unknown active accounts, IP risk
  - Allison to discuss with Adam today; tick-and-tie deletions to prior pilots/trials
- Retroactive AAR on all past pilots to consolidate learnings in one place

### Expedition and European BD Strategy

- Expedition (largest UK defense fund) flagged as under-leveraged relationship
  - Tad has the primary relationship; Allison reached out to Sophie in London office
  - BA Systems invested 30-40M into a Zodiac-linked company; Zodiac gone quiet but connection worth maintaining
  - Rory pursuing Oracle for similar reasons: non-competing product, Oracle has MOD contracts, SME program could open doors
- Colter tracking long-cycle “campaigns”: 20th Group, SOCOM TE-26 leads, Northern Strike threads
  - Functions like a capture manager role; needs visibility and a handoff point to sales
  - Need to define: how to socialize, when to kick to sales, how to capture post-conference leads in HubSpot

### Next Steps

- **Connect Marae's HubSpot commercial segment to dashboards** (Allison)
  - Post-call sync with Marae to find where the pipeline connections broke.
- **Send Reuters pre-call alignment email** (Colby)
  - Email Colter, Rob, and Reuters distro: scope is credentials + security only, official launch date is July 20th.
- **Connect all outbound activity to HubSpot** (Colby)
  - Cold emails, new leads, and Navy contacts not yet in the system.
- **Audit and clean up unknown production accounts** (Allison)
  - Discuss with Adam today; tie each deletion back to its pilot or trial and confirm loop was closed.
- **Pull Rory's bank record for ARCloud (DSTL SMT framework) application** (Allison)
  - Rory needs a suitable bank record to complete the submission.
- **Draft European travel targets for Q3/Q4** (Rory)
  - Bullet-point list of priority locations: Nordics, Poland, Estonia, Germany.
- **Explore Expedition relationship with Tad** (Allison)
  - Check sensitivity given fundraise, then assess portfolio intros and whether Rory can engage Sophie or Sam.
- **Map EOD Unit 3 paperwork next steps** (Colby)
  - Joey (ops master chief) to complete PMS 408 forms to initiate RCO funding approval.
